federalPrograms.name: Federal Pell Grant
federalPrograms.eligibility: Undergraduate students with financial need; no repayment required. Award based on SAI (Student Aid Index), cost of attendance, enrollment status.
federalPrograms.incomeLimit: Typically families under ~$60,000 qualify for some Pell; maximum for lowest-income families

federalPrograms.name: Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG)
federalPrograms.eligibility: Undergraduate students with exceptional financial need; Pell Grant recipients get priority. Limited funds — apply early.

federalPrograms.name: TEACH Grant
federalPrograms.eligibility: Students agreeing to teach high-need subjects in low-income schools for 4 years after graduation. Converts to loan if service requirement not met.
federalPrograms.condition: Must complete 4 years of qualifying teaching within 8 years

federalPrograms.name: Iraq and Afghanistan Service Grant
federalPrograms.eligibility: Students whose parent or guardian died as a result of military service in Iraq or Afghanistan after 9/11, and who are not Pell-eligible.

federalPrograms.name: Direct Subsidized Loan
federalPrograms.eligibility: Undergraduate students with demonstrated financial need. Government pays interest while in school at least half-time.

federalPrograms.name: Direct Unsubsidized Loan
federalPrograms.eligibility: Available to all students regardless of financial need. Interest accrues while in school.

federalPrograms.name: Direct PLUS Loan (Parent)
federalPrograms.description: Up to cost of attendance minus other financial aid received
federalPrograms.eligibility: Parents of dependent undergraduate students. Credit check required. No aggregate limit.

federalPrograms.name: Federal Work-Study
federalPrograms.description: Part-time jobs for undergraduate and graduate students with financial need. Federal funds cover 75% of wages. Award varies by school.
federalPrograms.eligibility: Students with financial need; limited funding — apply early via FAFSA.
